title: paspalum paniculatum subsp. umbrosum
intro: Paspalum paniculatum subsp. umbrosum, also known as umbrella grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of umbrella grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. It can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topicals, and is also consumed as a food source in some parts of the world.
